source.title: Structural Damage - Crane Auxiliary Jib Detachment

context.region: Gulf of Mexico - Eugene Island Block 252

event.top_event: Structural Damage - Crane Auxiliary Jib Detachment
actions_taken: Crane operator scheduled for Crane Operator & Rigging refresher training through different training program
Arena evaluating internal process with field leadership to improve selection and assignments of crane operators on Arena facilities
Crane placed out of service immediately
event.summary: On August 20, 2023, at approximately 1000 hours, the auxiliary jib on the platform crane detached while attempting to relocate a construction supply box weighing 3,886 lbs. The crane operator failed to center the auxiliary line over the construction supply box, causing it to become lodged on a capping beam. As the operator continued to lift, side loading caused the boom to twist and the bolts holding the auxiliary jib to shear. The jib detached and became entangled in hydraulic hose lines. No injuries occurred. Repairs totaled $80,000.

hazards.name: Crane lifting operations with heavy loads
Structural failure of crane boom and jib

threats.name: Improper centering of auxiliary line over load
Load becoming wedged or obstructed during lift
Continued lifting with side loading condition

consequences.name: Auxiliary jib bolts shear and jib detaches from boom
Detached jib becomes entangled in hydraulic hose lines
Crane equipment damage and loss of service
Potential personnel injury from falling jib

controls.name: Job Safety Analysis (JSA) for crane operations
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to identify and communicate safe lifting procedures

supporting_text: The Job Safety Analysis that was completed for the crane operations that morning discussed centering the crane over the lift to prevent side loading the crane.

controls.name: Crane operator centering procedure
controls.barrier_role: Operational control to ensure proper load positioning

supporting_text: The crane operator failed to center the auxiliary line over the construction supply box causing the supply box to become lodged on a capping beam.
The crane operator began to make the lift but failed to center the auxiliary line over the center of the construction supply box.

controls.name: Rigger communication and stop signal protocol
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to halt unsafe lifting operations

supporting_text: The rigger observed the lift but failed to communicate the "all stop signal" to the crane operator.
The rigger failed to communicate the "all stop signal" once he realized the construction supply box footing was wedged on a capping beam.

controls.name: Crane operator load monitoring and force assessment
controls.barrier_role: Operational control to detect excessive forces and side loading
controls.lod_basis: Operator skill and situational awareness during lifting

supporting_text: The crane operator also failed to determine the amount of force being applied to the crane boom while continuing to attempt the lift causing the auxiliary jib bolts to shear.

controls.name: Crane auxiliary jib structural integrity and bolt design
controls.barrier_role: Engineering control to prevent structural failure under side loading

supporting_text: The force caused the boom to twist and the bolts holding the auxiliary jib to shear.
Excessive force applied to the crane boom caused auxiliary jib bolts to shear.

controls.name: Hydraulic hose entanglement prevention
controls.barrier_role: Engineering control to prevent falling jib from reaching deck

supporting_text: The auxiliary jib detached from the boom and became entangled in the hydraulic hose lines which kept the auxiliary jib from falling to the deck.
The crane boom was placed over the heliport to prevent the auxiliary jib from falling to the deck.

controls.name: Crane operator training and competency
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to ensure operator knowledge and skill

supporting_text: Inadequate knowledge of equipment operation: The crane operator failed to center the auxiliary line over the construction supply box as well as applying enough force on the crane boom to shear the jib bolts once the construction supply box was wedged.
the crane operator had only one hitch at EI 252 I

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
